This measured live behavior is what the v1.30+ retests assume - do NOT change to placement-bar=1 arithmetic. Broker wall-clock expiry (+3d) remains the dead-EA backstop.' No code change.

downgraded.what: GV LEAK (hypothesized in the brief, confirmed): pending-ATR terminal globals MPB_v130_pending_atr_<orderTicket> are created in StorePendingSigAtr (line 2136) and deleted only via TakePendingSigAtr, which is reached exclusively through the RAM array g_pendingSigAtr (SweepStalePendingSigAtr iterates the array, not the GV namespace). After a restart the array is rebuilt by RestorePendingSigAtrFromLiveOrders (2155-2171) from LIVE orders only — so a pending cancelled/expired while the EA was offline leaves its GV orphaned forever (well, until MT5's 4-week unused-GV auto-purge). Orphans are trade-inert: PeekPendingSigAtr is only ever keyed by tickets of live orders or of entry deals of real positions, and a dead unfilled ticket can never match either.
downgraded.proposed_edit: Extend SweepStalePendingSigAtr with a throttled (e.g. once/hour static-datetime guard) namespace sweep: for i in GlobalVariablesTotal(), take names with prefix "MPB_v130_pending_atr_", parse the ticket; KEEP if ordInfo.Select(ticket) succeeds (live) or HistoryOrderSelect fails (history unsynced — retry later); if the order is in history with ORDER_POSITION_ID==0 (cancelled/expired unfilled) GlobalVariableDel it; if ORDER_POSITION_ID!=0, delete only when that position ID is no longer open on the account (FindOpenPositionById-style scan without the magic filter), otherwise leave it for the existing RegisterPositionState/TakePendingSigAtrForPosition recovery path.
downgraded.rationale: Pure persistence hygiene: every deletion condition provably corresponds to state the trading engine can never read again. The keep-guards replicate the existing sweep's resync caution so no restorable frozen ATR is ever lost (losing one would be batch C — hence the guards are mandatory, not optional).
downgraded.evidence: The GV leak is real (StorePendingSigAtr sets the GV at line 2136; the only deleters — TakePendingSigAtr 2150, called from the array-driven SweepStalePendingSigAtr 1516-1542, ManagePendingOrders 1603, CancelAllPendings 3143 — are all keyed by tickets the EA currently tracks, and RestorePendingSigAtrFromLiveOrders 2155-2171 rebuilds the array from live orders only, so an offline-cancelled pending orphans its GV). All three GV readers (PeekPendingSigAtr call sites 741, 2163, 2187; RegisterPositionState callers 754/826/1535/1684/2818 all operate on open positions or live fill events) are covered by the proposed keep-guards WITHIN one account, and MQL5's sequential event queue rules out the sweep racing the DEAL_ENTRY_IN read at 741. However, the edit is not batch-B: it mutates persisted state the trading engine reads back, and a concrete behavior change exists — MT5 globals are terminal-wide while tickets are per-account, so with two accounts in one terminal (user runs Deriv real + FTMO trial), account B's history can contain a cancelled unfilled order whose ticket number collides with account A's still-live pending; the namespace sweep on B then deletes A's frozen-ATR GV (ORDER_POSITION_ID==0 path), and after a restart on A the fill falls back to fill-time ATR (lines 752-753), changing riskPrice (2686-2687: InpStopAtrMult*signalAtr) and partial geometry on a real position. The current code can never delete a GV it did not place/restore on the current account, so this is a new loss path, violating the proposal's own "no restorable frozen ATR is ever lost" invariant — by the brief's own rationale that makes it batch C. To be safe even in batch C it needs a further guard before deletion: require HistoryOrderGetInteger(ticket, ORDER_MAGIC)==InpMagicNumber (and ideally account-login scoping in new GV names). Minor: the brief's cited lines 2516-2542 are partial-close retry code; the actual pending-ATR block is 2100-2198.

C.what: ConsecutiveLossesToday is recomputed from scratch every heartbeat scan pass: HistorySelect over the whole day plus an O(deals^2) inner loop (full re-scan of all deals per distinct position) and a FindOpenPositionById scan per exit deal. On a busy day this is the single most wasteful hot-path computation in the EA.
C.proposed_edit: IF ever optimized: cache the streak keyed on (g_currentDay, HistoryDealsTotal after the HistorySelect) and invalidate on day rollover or deal-count change. DO NOT apply in this cleanup.
C.rationale: The streak gates new entries (line 674). Any memoization changes when open-position exclusions and late-synced history are re-evaluated, and HistorySelect/HistorySelectByPosition global selection-context interactions make equivalence unprovable without a dedicated test. The recompute-fresh behavior is what has been running live; cost is bounded (runs at most once per heartbeat, only while not halted). Flagged for completeness, classified C, recommend leaving as-is.

C.what: KNOWN DEFECT (3) W2 candle filter silently PASSES on bad high/low data: STATUS = PRESENT. In DetectImpulse the wick check is wrapped in `if(high1 > 0.0 && low1 > 0.0)` (1074); when iHigh/iLow return 0/negative (unsynced history), the filter body is skipped and execution falls through to line 1090+ with the impulse ACCEPTED - the W2 filter is silently bypassed. Contrast the close-price path (1051-1056), which correctly skips with 'missing bar data'. Since W2 (InpMinAdvWickAtr=0.30) is part of the live signal definition, a data glitch admits trades the validated signal would reject.
C.proposed_edit: In the `if(InpCandleFilter && InpMinAdvWickAtr > 0.0 && (fallingFast || risingFast))` block, add an else-branch: if high1 <= 0.0 || low1 <= 0.0 then (if !forExit LogSkip(symbol, "missing bar data (W2 high/low)")) and return false.
C.rationale: Fail-closed removes real (rare) trades the current live build would take, so it changes trading behavior even though it is the obviously correct direction. Decision item, not cleanup.

C.what: KNOWN DEFECT (4) halted-day pre-loop returns skip the bar clock: STATUS = PRESENT. ScanAllOnNewBars returns before the per-symbol loop on: halted flags (670), static floor (671), max-DD (672), daily loss (673), consec-losses (674-675), daily fill cap (676-677). None of these paths advance g_lastScanBar[i], unlike the in-loop concurrency check (691-693) which deliberately consumes the bar first ('signals are dropped, never scanned late mid-bar'). When a pre-loop gate clears mid-bar - the live-reachable case is the consec-loss streak (InpMaxConsecLosses=4) ending when a winning position closes; halts clear only at midnight rollover, which is an H1 boundary and near-benign - the next heartbeat scans the stale bar mid-bar and can place a pullback limit anchored to a signal close up to ~55 min old, which the validated engine would have dropped. Same class, smaller window: Heartbeat's ledger-invalid early return (644-649) also skips clock consumption for the seconds the ledger restore is deferred.
C.proposed_edit: Restructure ScanAllOnNewBars: always run the per-symbol loop and consume the bar clock (lines 681-691) first, then apply the halt/streak/cap gates as in-loop `continue` after the consume (exactly the pattern already used for the concurrency cap at 691-693).
C.rationale: Changes which signals are scanned live (drops late mid-bar scans the current build performs). It moves live TOWARD validated-engine semantics, but it is a trade-distribution change and must be a deliberate decision.

C.what: NEW DEFECT (same class as list item 3): the news guard checks only SYMBOL_CURRENCY_PROFIT (line 908). For USDJPY (profit currency JPY) HIGH-impact USD events - NFP, CPI, FOMC, exactly the events the 3-min guard exists for - do NOT block entries; each index CFD is likewise covered for a single currency. The guard is live (InpNewsBlockMins=3) but structurally half-blind on the FX symbol.
C.proposed_edit: Query CalendarValueHistory for both SYMBOL_CURRENCY_PROFIT and SYMBOL_CURRENCY_BASE (dedupe when equal) and block on a HIGH-impact hit in either.
C.rationale: Widening the guard blocks entries the current live build takes (a validated-distribution-altering protective gate, per the file's own line 279 taxonomy). Decision item.

C.what: NEW (borderline, halt-ordering nuance): the hard-halt latches are evaluated only AFTER `if(g_halted || g_haltedHard) return;` (670). While a daily-loss halt is active, a static-floor or max-DD breach does not latch g_haltedHard until the day rolls over and one more heartbeat runs; if equity recovers above the floor before rollover, the persistent hard halt is never latched at all. Practical exposure is small (both halt types already cancel pendings and block entries; only the never-auto-clear persistence differs), but the ordering contradicts the intent that the static floor is a hard line.
C.proposed_edit: Evaluate StaticFloorBreached()/DrawdownExceeded() and latch g_haltedHard BEFORE the g_halted early return (i.e., hoist lines 671-672 above line 670).
C.rationale: Changes when the persistent hard halt latches, which changes whether the EA trades on subsequent days after an intraday floor touch - risk-logic behavior, decision required.
